title: Write tests to expose bugs in a statistics module
instruction: stats.py contains several bugs in its functions (mean, median, mode, std_dev, percentile). Do NOT fix the code. Instead, write test_stats.py with tests that expose these bugs. Each test should directly assert the EXPECTED correct behavior (e.g., assert median([1,2,3,4]) == 2.5). Do NOT use try/except to catch errors — that masks the bug. Good tests will FAIL when run against the buggy code because they expect correct behavior that the code doesn't deliver. Run verify_tests.py to check your tests.
